name: Tormes River and Sierra de Gredos Loop
name_original: Por el Tormes observando la sierra — Sierra de Gredos
description: This moderate 13.6-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Gredos follows the Tormes River with panoramic views of Circo de Gredos.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Navarredonda De Gredos offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, characterized by a granite massif with peaks exceeding 2,500 meters. The area features well-preserved glacial formations, extensive pine forests, and riverine paths along the Tormes River. Routes often follow old tracks and trails, providing a mix of ascents and descents through varied landscapes. This region provides a natural backdrop for mountain biking, with options ranging from forest paths to routes with panoramic views of the Gredos mountains. Best mountain bike trails around Navarredonda De Gredos The most popular mountain bike trail is El río Tormes y las Vistas a la Sierra — Sierra de Gredos, a 31.2 miles (50.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers expansive views of the Sierra de Gredos and follows sections of the Tormes River. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tormes River and Sierra de Gredos Loop, a moderate 13.6 miles (22.0 km) path. This route provides scenic views of the Gredos mountains and follows the Tormes River, offering a mix of terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the GR 10 Trail: Stage 33 - Hoyocasero to Tamborrios, a 16.4 miles (26.4 km) trail leading through pine forests and connecting villages, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Mountain biking around Navarredonda De Gredos is defined by granite massifs, extensive pine forests, and the clear waters of the Tormes River. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Navarredonda De Gredos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 9 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Navarredonda De Gredos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Navarredonda De Gredos?
A: There are nearly 50 mountain bike trails around Navarredonda De Gredos, offering a range of experiences from moderate forest paths to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.6 stars.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Navarredonda De Gredos?
A: The terrain around Navarredonda De Gredos is diverse, featuring old natural paths, forest tracks through extensive pine forests, and scenic routes along the Tormes River. You'll encounter a mix of tracks, trails, and roads, often with granite formations characteristic of the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the area?
A: Yes, the region offers routes suitable for various skill levels. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly detailed, the presence of moderate forest paths suggests options that could be enjoyed by families. Look for routes with less elevation gain and smoother surfaces for a more relaxed experience.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking?
A: You can expect to see stunning natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5758520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes River&lt;/a&gt; with its clear waters and granite formations, and the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5754260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Paredes&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for extensive pine forests and offers panoramic views of the Sierra de Gredos, including the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural swimming spots near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the area features natural swimming spots. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6213569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of Hoyos del Espino&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, Las Chorreras, a spectacular waterfall near the Tormes' source, is also popular for cooling off in summer.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Navarredonda De Gredos?
A: While summer is an option, spring and autumn are highly recommended for mountain biking in Navarredonda De Gredos. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant with seasonal colors, making for a more comfortable and scenic ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379821126/tormes-river-and-sierra-de-gredos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes River and Sierra de Gredos Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22 km path that offers scenic views and follows the Tormes River. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31914832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Seis Ojos – Pozo de las Paredes loop from Navarredonda de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, covering about 44.6 km.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail in the area?
A: The longest mountain bike trail listed is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379091433/el-rio-tormes-y-las-vistas-a-la-sierra-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El río Tormes y las Vistas a la Sierra — Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult route spans 50.2 km and typically takes around 4 hours and 27 minutes to complete, offering expansive views and following sections of the Tormes River.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for advanced riders?
A: Yes, the region offers challenging routes for advanced riders. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379091433/el-rio-tormes-y-las-vistas-a-la-sierra-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El río Tormes y las Vistas a la Sierra — Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31914832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Seis Ojos – Pozo de las Paredes loop from Navarredonda de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Navarredonda De Gredos?
A: The komoot community rates the mountain bike trails in Navarredonda De Gredos highly, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning panoramic views of the Sierra de Gredos, and the scenic routes through extensive pine forests and along the Tormes River.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through pine forests?
A: Yes, many routes in the area traverse beautiful pine forests. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379841602/gr-10-trail-stage-33-hoyocasero-to-tamborrios" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 10 Trail: Stage 33 - Hoyocasero to Tamborrios&lt;/a&gt; leads through extensive pine forests, connecting villages and offering shaded sections.
tips: On the banks of the Tormes River, surrounded by a pine forest, the Hoyos del Espino Natural Pool is a must-see on a warm summer day. Please check local information for opening hours.

A small town in the Tormes valley and one of the most direct accesses to the Sierra de Gredos Natural Park. As such, the city is very well positioned to support adventure tourism, with many campsites, hotels and restaurants. A great place to prepare for your hike.
There are beautiful trails here and plenty of opportunities to enjoy the recreation area

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Por el Tormes mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is approximately 22 km long and generally takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes to complete. However, your actual duration may vary depending on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and trail conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Por el Tormes trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily follows tracks and paths along the Tormes River and through pine forests. You can expect a mix of gentle ascents and descents. While generally moderate, some sections may present technical challenges or areas with loose cattle, requiring good fitness and mountain biking skills.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers expansive panoramic views of the Circo de Gredos. You'll ride along the Tormes River, through beautiful pine forests, and past diverse natural environments. A notable highlight is the Sierra de Gredos viewpoint. You might also pass by "Las Chorreras," a spectacular waterfall and popular swimming area.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to mountain bike Por el Tormes observando la sierra?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gredos region is beautiful year-round, but for mountain biking, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be hot, especially in the river valleys, while winter may bring snow to higher elevations, impacting trail accessibility.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Por el Tormes mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially due to the presence of wildlife and potential loose cattle along the route. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to mountain bike in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Parque Regional de la Sierra de Gredos. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and stay on marked paths.

faqs.question: What is the starting point and parking situation for this trail?
faqs.answer: This route often starts from towns like Hoyos del Espino, Navarredonda de Gredos, or El Barco de Ávila. Parking availability will depend on your chosen starting point within these towns. It's advisable to check local parking options in advance.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate in difficulty, requiring good fitness. While it features gentle ascents and descents, there can be challenging sections with technical terrain. Beginners with some prior mountain biking experience and good physical condition might manage, but absolute novices may find parts difficult.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails in the Sierra de Gredos?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of official trails such as De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa 34), A vueltas con Gredos, A vueltas con Gredos-MTB, and Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda, among others. This offers opportunities to extend or modify your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any opportunities for wild swimming along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes along the Tormes River, and a notable spot mentioned is "Las Chorreras," which is a spectacular waterfall and popular swimming area. The Natural Pool of Hoyos del Espino is also a highlight along the way, offering another potential spot for a refreshing dip.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking here?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Gredos region is rich in wildlife. Along this trail, you might spot various bird species, including golden eagles. The wider Gredos area is also known for its Spanish ibex. Keep an eye out for diverse flora and fauna as you ride through the natural environments.

waypoints.name: Natural Pool of Hoyos del Espino
